title: Tablet III — The Forest Journey (Departure)
source: CDLI tablet photograph (P395404) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
description: Tablet III — The Forest Journey (Departure) of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Preparations for the journey to the Cedar Forest; Ninsun's prayer; the elders' counsel. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P395404). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).
introduction:
This entry presents Tablet III — The Forest Journey (Departure) of the Epic of Gilgamesh — the oldest major work of literature, composed in Akkadian cuneiform around 2100-1200 BCE.

  1. Tablet photograph: Cuneiform tablet photo from CDLI (artifact P395404), showing the actual clay tablet with the wedge-shaped script.
  2. Original text: Akkadian transliteration from Langdon's scholarly edition (public domain).
  3. English translation: Langdon's English rendering (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

translation.original_ref: Tablet III — The Forest Journey (Departure), section 1
translation.original_text:
Anunit, goddess, 158, 12; 166, 2.

_apunnatu,_ nostrils, _pitik, apunnati_, 217, 28.

_araku_, be pale, Prt. _iriku_, 217, 31.

translation.original_ref: Tablet III — The Forest Journey (Departure), section 2
translation.original_text:
Aruru, goddess. Lamentation to, 115. Sister of Enlil, 115, 2; 171, 29; 190, 25. Other references, 116, 13:15:18; 117, 34 f.

Asarludug, god, 163, 8; 170, 4.

As-im-ur, title of Moon-god, 136, 12. _as_ omitted, No. 19, 2.

translation.original_text:
